title: canlib32 shim: 13 exports + UDP/TCP cannelloni transport
description: Drop-in canlib32.dll implementing the 13 resolved Kvaser symbols; winsock RX thread + ring; UDP datagram and TCP (CANNELLONIv1 handshake) transports; env config.
close_reason: Shim implements all 13 exports + UDP and TCP cannelloni transports; builds clean 32/64-bit

title: cannelloni wire codec + host interop test
description: Pure-C codec (encodeFrame/decodeFrame, UDP buildPacket/parsePacket) + Kvaser<->SocketCAN id translation, compiling under both mingw and native gcc. Host test cross-validates against cannelloni's own parser.cpp/decoder.cpp.
close_reason: Codec done; cross-validated vs cannelloni's own parser.cpp/decoder.cpp in all 4 directions, RESULT: PASS

title: canbridge.py: harden for multi-app/multi-thread use (FD, error resilience, loop guard)
description: Audit of new userspace CAN bridge found: (1) classic-only sockets silently drop CAN FD frames (project is FD-heavy); (2) pump threads have no try/except so any transient send/recv error silently kills one direction; (3) duplicate bridges on the same iface-pair create an infinite amplification loop; (4) blocking send couples directions and stalls shutdown; (5) bare ImportError; (6) partial-open socket leak; (7) no observability. Apply: fd=True, resilient pump w/ counters + transition logging, flock per-pair loop guard (--force override), send timeout, friendly import error, partial-open cleanup, optional --stats heartbeat + final summary.

title: GitHub Actions release CI: tag-triggered Windows DLL builds (32/64-bit) + release with sha256sums
description: On pushing a v* tag, cross-compile canlib32.dll for i686 and x86_64 windows-gnu targets, attach both DLLs plus SHA256SUMS.txt to a GitHub release. Repo: aalarchiv/kvasilloni. Cut v0.2.0.

title: Replace non-ASCII chars in docs/sources (em-dashes, arrows, box-drawing)
description: Project docs and Rust/C sources use UTF-8 punctuation (em/en dashes, arrows, ellipsis, middle dot, box-drawing glyphs) that may not render for readers without full UTF-8 support. Replace with plain ASCII equivalents in our own files; leave vendored .beads/ and license COPYING* untouched.
close_reason: Replaced em/en dashes, arrows, ellipsis, approx, middle dot, and box-drawing glyphs with ASCII across our docs and sources; vendored .beads/ and COPYING* left untouched; tests pass.

title: CAN-FD through canWrite + INI config
description: Wire CAN-FD send (FDF/BRS/ESI, 64-byte payloads, DLC rounding) and add Windows-native INI config (kvasilloni.ini, env override).
close_reason: Done. canWrite FD path (fd_round_len + BRS/ESI mapping), canRead reports FDF/BRS/ESI. INI config via src/config.rs (defaults<ini<env), auto-discovered next to DLL/exe via GetModuleFileNameW. 11 unit tests + e2e selftest PASS x3 (classic UDP/TCP, FD+BRS over UDP, INI-only no-env).

title: README + Windows-VM deployment docs
description: Architecture diagram, build, env vars, both transports, cannelloni CLI for each side, drop-in deploy steps.
close_reason: README written: architecture, build, Windows deploy + env vars, cannelloni CLI for UDP/TCP, selftest, wire-format reference.

title: wine + vcan end-to-end selftest
description: Build cannelloni from refs, bring up vcan0, run cannelloni (UDP and TCP), run canshim_probe.exe under wine, assert frames cross both directions. Skip gracefully without root/wine.
close_reason: Rust shim e2e selftest PASS: both UDP and TCP, both directions, through wine + stock cannelloni + isolated vcan1. Found cannelloni TCP server needs -R or -p (peer check).

title: Makefile: 32+64-bit DLL build, export verify
description: Build i686 and x86_64 canlib32.dll via mingw with --kill-at undecorated stdcall exports; targets: all, test, verify (objdump exports), selftest.
close_reason: Makefile builds both bitnesses with undecorated exports; make verify confirms all 13 names, no @N decoration

title: Implement channel enumeration: canGetNumberOfChannels / canGetChannelData
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first). HARDEST in the set.

Signatures:
  canStatus __stdcall canGetNumberOfChannels(int *channelCount);
  canStatus __stdcall canGetChannelData(int channel, int item, void *buffer, size_t bufsize);

WHY: apps that SCAN/LIST adapters (instead of opening a fixed channel from the UI) call these. The current app does NOT (PGN_TRANSPORT.md 1.1), so this is purely for retargeting.

DIRECTIONS:
- Decide the channel model. Simplest: expose a fixed channel count from config (e.g. KVASILLONI_CHANNELS / ini `channels`, default 1) so canOpenChannel(channel) maps to host vcan<channel> per existing convention.
- canGetNumberOfChannels: write that count to *channelCount, return canOK.
- canGetChannelData: switch on `item` (canCHANNELDATA_* enum in canlib.h). Implement at least:
    canCHANNELDATA_CHANNEL_NAME (string), canCHANNELDATA_DEVDESCR_ASCII, canCHANNELDATA_CARD_SERIAL_NO,
    canCHANNELDATA_CHANNEL_CAP / canCHANNELDATA_CARD_TYPE as needed.
  Fill `buffer` (bufsize-bounded) with synthetic-but-plausible values (e.g. name "kvasilloni vcan{channel}"). Return canOK for handled items, canERR_PARAM for unhandled.
- Pull the exact item enum + expected buffer types from refs/kvaser_canlib/.../canlib.h (search canCHANNELDATA_) and refs/pycanlib/canlib/canlib/enums.py (ChannelData).
- Add both names to Makefile EXPORTS.
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green; a probe that enumerates prints count + a channel name. Document the item coverage in README Scope.

title: Implement blocking I/O: canReadWait / canReadSync / canWriteWait / canWriteSync
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first).

Signatures:
  canStatus __stdcall canReadWait(CanHandle h, long *id, void *msg, unsigned *dlc, unsigned *flag, unsigned long *time, unsigned long timeout);
  canStatus __stdcall canReadSync(CanHandle h, unsigned long timeout);
  canStatus __stdcall canWriteWait(CanHandle h, long id, void *msg, unsigned dlc, unsigned flag, unsigned long timeout);
  canStatus __stdcall canWriteSync(CanHandle h, unsigned long timeout);
(timeout is in ms; 0xFFFFFFFF/canWAIT_INFINITE = block forever; 0 = poll-once.)

DIRECTIONS:
- canReadWait: like canRead but if the ring is empty, wait up to `timeout` ms for a frame, else return canERR_NOMSG. Cleanest impl: add a Condvar to the RX ring in src/transport.rs (replace Mutex<VecDeque> usage with Mutex<VecDeque>+Condvar; rx thread notify_one on push) and `read_wait(&self, Duration) -> Option<Frame>`. Keep non-blocking read() too.
- canReadSync: wait up to timeout for a frame to be AVAILABLE without removing it (peek). Return canOK if one arrives, else canERR_NOMSG.
- canWriteWait: we send synchronously already; implement as canWrite ignoring timeout (return its status). 
- canWriteSync: no TX queue -> return canOK immediately.
- Add all four names to Makefile EXPORTS.

TEST: extend test/canshim_probe.c with a `--wait` mode that calls canReadWait(timeout=2000) and assert it blocks-then-returns the injected frame; add a selftest case.
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green; canReadWait returns the injected frame within timeout, canERR_NOMSG on timeout.

title: Implement canSetBusOutputControl / canGetBusOutputControl
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first).

Signatures:
  canStatus __stdcall canSetBusOutputControl(const CanHandle hnd, const unsigned int drivertype);
  canStatus __stdcall canGetBusOutputControl(const CanHandle hnd, unsigned int *drivertype);

DIRECTIONS (benign stub, like canSetBusParams):
- Store the last drivertype in a global (static Mutex<u32> or AtomicU32 in lib.rs) on Set; return it on Get.
- canDRIVER_* constants are in canlib.h (e.g. canDRIVER_NORMAL=4, canDRIVER_SILENT=1); default to canDRIVER_NORMAL.
- Set: accept any value, store, return canOK. Get: write stored value (or NORMAL default) to *drivertype if non-null, return canOK.
- Add both names to Makefile EXPORTS.
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green; Get returns what Set stored.

title: Implement canFlushReceiveQueue / canFlushTransmitQueue
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first).

Signatures (refs/.../canlib.h):
  canStatus __stdcall canFlushReceiveQueue(const CanHandle hnd);
  canStatus __stdcall canFlushTransmitQueue(const CanHandle hnd);

DIRECTIONS (easy first issue, REAL behavior):
- In src/lib.rs add both exports (extern "system", c_int handle -> c_int).
- canFlushReceiveQueue: clear the RX ring. Add `pub fn clear_rx(&self)` to Conn in src/transport.rs that locks `rx` and calls `.clear()` on the VecDeque; call it from the export. Return canOK.
- canFlushTransmitQueue: we send synchronously (no TX queue), so it is a no-op. Return canOK.
- Add both names to Makefile EXPORTS.

TEST: unit-ish/e2e - in test/selftest.sh add a tiny check or just rely on make verify for presence; optionally extend canshim_probe.c to call flush after a burst. 
ACCEPTANCE: make verify lists both names undecorated; make test green; clear_rx empties the ring (a follow-up read returns canERR_NOMSG).

title: Expand canlib32 export coverage (retargeting to other CANlib apps)
description:
WHY: The shim currently exports only the 13 functions the czone configuration-tool.exe resolves. To run a DIFFERENT Windows CANlib app against a Linux vcan, its imported symbols must all be exported (see AGENTS.md AI-FIRST NOTE: run a coverage check first). These child issues implement the functions a coverage check is most likely to surface. None are needed for the current app; do them on demand when retargeting.

=== ONBOARDING (read this first in a new session) ===
Layout:
  src/lib.rs       - the #[no_mangle] pub extern "system" exports (the FFI surface). ADD NEW FUNCTIONS HERE.
  src/wire.rs      - cannelloni codec + Kvaser<->SocketCAN translation + constants (canMSG_*, CAN_*). Has unit tests.
  src/transport.rs - Conn: UDP/TCP transport, background RX thread, bounded ring (read()/write()).
  src/config.rs    - layered config (defaults < kvasilloni.ini < KVASILLONI_* env).
  test/canshim_probe.c - wine loader that LoadLibrary+GetProcAddress the DLL (mimics a real app).
  test/selftest.sh - e2e over isolated vcan1 + stock cannelloni (classic UDP/TCP, FD, INI cases).
  reference/c-prototype/ - frozen original C impl (do not edit).
Signatures/constants come from refs/kvaser_canlib/include/kvaser_canlib/{canlib.h,canstat.h} and refs/pycanlib/canlib/canlib/enums.py.

Build/test loop:
  make            # build 32+64-bit DLL (targets i686/x86_64-pc-windows-gnu)
  make test       # host unit tests (cargo test)
  make verify     # asserts the exported names are present + undecorated
  make selftest   # full wine+cannelloni+vcan1 e2e (needs wine, can-utils, cmake, sudo for vcan)

HOW TO ADD AN EXPORT (mechanical):
  1. Add `#[no_mangle] pub extern "system" fn canXxx(...) -> c_int { guard(|| { ... }) }` in src/lib.rs.
     Use exact arg types via std::os::raw (c_int/c_long/c_uint/c_ulong/c_void/c_char). __stdcall is `extern "system"`.
  2. Add the new name to the EXPORTS list in the Makefile `verify` target so `make verify` checks it.
     (No .def file: undecorated 32-bit exports come from -Wl,--kill-at in .cargo/config.toml.)
  3. Return codes: canOK=0, canERR_PARAM=-1, canERR_NOMSG=-2, canERR_NOTFOUND=-3 (already consts in lib.rs).
  4. Wrap bodies in guard() so panics never cross FFI. Global state: CONN: Mutex<Option<Conn>>.
  5. Add/extend a test/selftest case where behavior is observable; run make test + make verify (+ make selftest if data-path).

Verify a target needs a function: AGENTS.md top has the coverage-check procedure (objdump static imports + strings for dynamic GetProcAddress + KVASILLONI_LOG runtime log).

title: Implement canSetNotify (event-driven callbacks)
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first). Most complex; do last.

Signature:
  canStatus __stdcall canSetNotify(const CanHandle hnd, void (*callback)(canNotifyData*), unsigned int notifyFlags, void *tag);
  (canNotifyData layout + canNOTIFY_* flags in refs/.../canlib.h.)

WHY: apps that want async RX/TX/status notifications instead of polling. Current app polls, so retargeting-only.

DIRECTIONS:
- Store callback ptr + notifyFlags + tag per handle (global in lib.rs). Treat the C fn ptr as a usize you stash (it is NOT Send by default - wrap carefully; document the assumption that the app keeps the callback valid until canClose/canSetNotify(NULL)).
- In the RX thread (src/transport.rs), when a frame is pushed and notifyFlags & canNOTIFY_RX is set, invoke the callback with a populated canNotifyData. Must define a #[repr(C)] canNotifyData mirroring canlib.h exactly.
- Marshal carefully: the callback runs on OUR rx thread; keep it short. Provide a way to clear (callback NULL).
- CAUTION: calling back into managed/.NET code from a native thread can need care; verify under wine with a probe that registers a callback and counts invocations.
- Add the name to Makefile EXPORTS.
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green; a probe registering a canNOTIFY_RX callback receives N callbacks for N injected frames. Document threading caveats in README.

title: Implement canIoCtl (control-op dispatch)
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first).

Signature:
  canStatus __stdcall canIoCtl(const CanHandle hnd, unsigned int func, void *buf, unsigned int buflen);

WHY: catch-all control entry point; different apps use different canIOCTL_* sub-functions.

DIRECTIONS:
- Implement as a match on `func` (canIOCTL_* enum from refs/.../canlib.h - search canIOCTL_; cross-check refs/pycanlib enums Ioctl).
- Handle the common, cheap ones; return canOK and zero/sane out-values:
    canIOCTL_FLUSH_RX_BUFFER -> clear RX ring (reuse Conn::clear_rx from the flush issue),
    canIOCTL_FLUSH_TX_BUFFER -> no-op,
    canIOCTL_GET_TIMER_SCALE / SET_TIMER_SCALE, canIOCTL_SET_TXACK -> accept/return canOK,
    canIOCTL_GET_DEVICE_HANDLE etc -> canERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED (define const = -32 per canstat.h) if unknown.
- Bound all reads/writes to buf by buflen.
- Add the name to Makefile EXPORTS.
NOTE: depends conceptually on the flush issue (shares clear_rx). 
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green; unknown func returns a defined error, not a crash.

title: Implement canAccept / canObjBufSetFilter (acceptance filtering)
description:
Parent epic: kvasilloni-5yp (read its ONBOARDING first).

Signatures:
  canStatus __stdcall canAccept(const CanHandle hnd, const long envelope, const unsigned int flag);
  canStatus __stdcall canObjBufSetFilter(const CanHandle hnd, int idx, unsigned int code, unsigned int mask);

DIRECTIONS:
- Two valid levels of effort:
  (a) BENIGN STUB: accept args, return canOK, do no filtering (all frames still delivered). Fine if the target app only sets filters as a formality. Start here.
  (b) REAL: store (code,mask) filters per handle and apply them in Conn::read()/the RX push path in src/transport.rs, dropping frames whose can_id & mask != code & mask. canAccept flag selects set/clear and std/ext (see canFILTER_* in canlib.h).
- flag values + envelope semantics: refs/kvaser_canlib/.../canlib.h (search canFILTER_), refs/pycanlib enums.
- Add both names to Makefile EXPORTS.
ACCEPTANCE: make verify + make test green. If REAL: add a selftest case injecting two IDs and asserting only the accepted one reaches canRead.
